visual_checks: Trigger interactive elements (buttons, scrolling, hovering) and observe if motion animations occur
Check for parallax scrolling effects that move background content at different speeds
Look for elements that animate when they enter the viewport during scrolling
Identify zoom, slide, spin, or bounce animations triggered by user interaction
Check if a user preference or setting exists to disable non-essential motion animations
Observe whether the page respects the prefers-reduced-motion media query
pass_conditions: No motion animations are triggered by user interactions
Motion animations triggered by interactions can be disabled via a site setting or toggle
The page respects the prefers-reduced-motion media query and suppresses non-essential animations
All interaction-triggered animations are essential to the functionality being conveyed
fail_conditions: User interactions trigger motion animations with no mechanism to disable them
Parallax scrolling effects are present with no way to turn them off
Elements slide, zoom, spin, or bounce on interaction and prefers-reduced-motion is not respected
Hover or focus effects include motion animations that cannot be disabled
na_conditions: Page contains no interaction-triggered motion animations of any kind
ne_conditions: AI response could not be parsed
Screenshots could not be captured for visual verification
Interactive behavior could not be triggered to evaluate animation responses
common_mistakes: Parallax scrolling that cannot be turned off and is purely decorative
Scroll-triggered slide-in or fade-in animations without a reduced-motion fallback
Hover animations that include movement (not just color changes) with no disable option
Using CSS transitions with transform properties without a prefers-reduced-motion media query
Confusing opacity/color transitions (generally acceptable) with motion animations (position, scale, rotation)

examples.pass: A page has scroll-triggered slide-in animations but includes a @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) rule that disables all transform and translate animations, replacing them with simple opacity fades
examples.fail: Cards zoom and rotate into view as the user scrolls down the page, and there is no setting or reduced-motion query to disable these motion effects
examples.partial: The site respects prefers-reduced-motion for most animations but a hero section parallax scrolling effect still triggers motion even when reduced motion is preferred

plain_meaning: Motion animation triggered by user interaction (parallax, scroll-linked movement, hover-triggered transitions) must be disableable by the user, unless the animation is essential to the function or information. The intent is to protect users with vestibular disorders from nausea-inducing motion.
scope_applies_to: Hover or click animations that move content across the screen
Transition animations between routes or views
Decorative motion that plays on interaction
scope_does_not_apply_to: Animation essential to understanding content (e.g. a progress indicator, a tutorial demo)
Non-motion animations (color fades, opacity changes with no movement)
Auto-playing motion not triggered by interaction (that is SC 2.2.2)
borderline_calibration: Honoring prefers-reduced-motion media query is a strong signal of compliance.
A setting in the UI to disable animation also satisfies the SC.
Essential animations (loading spinners, progress bars) are exempt.
If no interaction-triggered motion exists, N/A.
